commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=93935075245b09018b0593fb4aa9c0d4e84f6ea7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes prose-token cron denial classification, adds `errorCode` to tool error summaries, extracts structured tool error codes from failed tool results, and updates regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source and tests show `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` promotes denial-looking prose to fatal cron output; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer review to fix real thrown-error code propagation and add real cron proof; automation should not take over while contributor proof is missing.

target.title: fix(cron): use structured denial signals
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove cron outcome classification based on denial tokens found in summary, output text, final assistant text, or payload prose.
- Preserve fatal cron denial handling through typed `failureSignal` metadata sourced from structured tool error codes.
- Add regression coverage so prose-only denial text is not promoted to fatal, while structured denial signals still are.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron diagnostic classification no longer treats ordinary assistant/tool prose containing denial-looking phrases as a fatal cron failure unless structured error metadata marks the run as fatal.

Real environment: local OpenClaw checkout in an isolated worktree on this branch.

Exact commands after the patch:

```sh
pnpm install
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/failure-signal.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:changed
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

- Focused vitest lane passed: 6 files, 141 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed the core/coreTests lane, lint, typechecks, dependency guards, import-cycle guard, and related changed-file checks.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result: prose-only `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED` / `could not run` text remains normal cron output, prose-only tool output does not synthesize an error code, and typed structured denial metadata still produces a fatal cron failure.

Not tested: full baseline `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; this used the focused core lane for the touched classifier and tool-error surfaces.

## Review

`codex review --base origin/main` was attempted twice locally, but the local Codex CLI failed before review started with OpenAI API 401 auth errors. Manual review caught and fixed one extraction bug where a string `details.error` could have been mistaken for a code.

## AI-assisted

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the diff, understand the code path, and ran the proof above locally.
target.author: abnershang

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
